summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the decay factor when a quantity decreases by 10% per year?

The decay factor is 1 − r = 1 − 0.10 = 0.90, which is the multiplier applied each year.

A car worth $30,000 depreciates 20% per year. What is it worth after 1 year?

After 1 year the value is 30,000 × (1 − 0.20) = 30,000 × 0.80 = $24,000.

In the model A = A₀(1 − r)ᵗ, if A₀ = 500 and r = 0.25, what is A after 1 time period?

A = 500(1 − 0.25)¹ = 500(0.75) = 375.

What is the y-intercept of f(x) = 500(0.7)ˣ?

The y-intercept is f(0) = 500(0.7)⁰ = 500 × 1 = 500.

A population decreases from 1,000 to 500 in 3 years. What is the half-life of this population?

The population was halved (from 1,000 to 500) in 3 years, so the half-life is 3 years.

A $10,000 investment loses 5% of its value each year. What is it worth after 2 years?

After 2 years the value is 10,000(0.95)² = 10,000 × 0.9025 = $9,025.

What is exponential decay?

Exponential decay is a proportional decrease where the quantity drops by the same percentage (not amount) in each equal time interval.

What is a half-life?

A half-life is the time required for a quantity to decrease to exactly 50% of its starting value.

What is a decay factor in the model A = A₀(1 − r)ᵗ?

The decay factor is (1 − r), the constant multiplier by which the quantity is reduced each time period.

What is the horizontal asymptote of the basic decay function f(x) = a · bˣ where 0 < b < 1 and a > 0?

As x increases, bˣ approaches 0 but never reaches it, so the horizontal asymptote is y = 0.

What is carbon-14 dating?

Carbon-14 dating estimates the age of once-living material by measuring how much carbon-14 has decayed, using its known half-life of about 5,730 years.

What is the difference between exponential decay and linear decay?

In exponential decay a fixed proportion is removed each period (e.g., 10%), while in linear decay a fixed amount is subtracted each period (e.g., 5 units).

A car is purchased for $25,000 and depreciates at 20% per year. What is its value after 2 years?

Value = 25,000(0.80)² = 25,000 × 0.64 = $16,000.

A computer purchased for $2,000 loses 30% of its value each year. What is its value after 3 years?

Value = 2,000(0.70)³ = 2,000 × 0.343 = $686.

A hot object at 180°F is placed in a 60°F room. If the temperature difference between the object and the room halves every 10 minutes, what is the object's temperature after 20 minutes?

Initial difference = 180 − 60 = 120°F. After 20 min (2 half-lives): difference = 120 × (1/2)² = 30°F. Temperature = 60 + 30 = 90°F.
